public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as door warden.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Balances command voice with practiced observation, sounding like someone who expects trouble to hide inside normality. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a door warden still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of door warden in and around The Rusted Loom Tavern, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep The Rusted Loom Tavern workable through the obligations of door warden while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must notice trouble early enough that force remains legitimate rather than merely reactive. Around The Rusted Loom Tavern, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of door warden visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Vera Wren can draw the party into contraband, false papers, or a suspicious cargo problem tied to The Rusted Loom Tavern.
Any task accepted from Vera Wren may turn into an escort, checkpoint, or route-control problem before the destination is reached.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Uniform, posture, and the way others make room mark Vera Wren as visible enforcement before rank ever needs to be named.

encounter_context.usual_environment: The Rusted Loom Tavern: checkpoints, barracks, patrol seams, or visible trouble-spots
encounter_context.interaction_anchor: threshold control or rapid incident response
